name: Stuffed Chayote (Creole Style)
recipeIngredient: 8 medium chayote squash (about 6 to 8 ounces each)
3/4 cup green onion with greens, finely chopped
1/2 cup red and/or green bell pepper, diced
2 medium tomatoes, peeled and seeded, chopped
3/4 pound raw shrimp, peeled and deveined, coarsely chopped
3 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
Known in some areas as vegetable pear or christophine, and as mirliton in Louisiana, delicate-tasting chayotes (Sechium edule) are superb when stuffed New Orleans Creole style. They're also wonderful sliced, steamed until crisp tender, and dressed, along with their edible single large seed, in a mustard vinaigrette.
1. Cut off and discard the stem end from each squash. Slice the vegetable in half lengthwise.
2. Remove seeds and use in another dish if desired. If necessary, cut a thin slice off the rounded side of each half so it will sit level.
3. Place halves on a steamer rack set over simmering water, cover, and steam until crisp-tender, about 20 minutes. Remove from steamer and let stand until cool enough to handle.
4. Using a spoon, scoop out the flesh from each squash half into a bowl, leaving a shell about 1/4 inch thick; reserve.
5. STUFFING: Heat 3 tablespoons oil and butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the yellow and green onions, sweet pepper, and celery, and sauté until the vegetables are soft, about 5 minutes.
6. Add the garlic and sauté 1 minute. Stir in the tomatoes, reserved squash pulp, parsley, thyme, and bay leaf, and continue to cook until all the vegetables are almost tender, about 5 minutes longer.
7. Reduce heat to medium-low, stir in the shrimp pieces, and cook until they turn bright pink. Stir in cooked crab and breadcrumbs, and season to taste with salt and black pepper and cayenne pepper.
8. Mound the stuffing mixture into the reserved squash halves. Dot the tops with butter and place in a greased baking dish. Bake until the tops are golden brown, about 25 minutes.
9. Garnish with thyme sprigs and lemon wedges and serve piping hot.
